name: Muraqqa ibn Thumama
title: Brave Asadi
shortDescription: A brave man who rose to defend the Imam in Karbala but was arrested by his tribe and exiled by order of Ibn Ziyad.
fullStory:
Muraqqa ibn Thumama al-Asadi was a brave man present in Karbala on the day of Ashura. Seeing Imam Hussain's (AS) loneliness and the oppression of the Kufan army, he decided to rise in defense of the Prophet's (PBUH) son.

Muraqqa took up arms and entered the battlefield to help the Imam, but since his tribe (Bani Asad) was present in Umar Sa'd's army, a group of his relatives attacked and arrested him.

To save his life, they chained him and took him to Umar Sa'd. Umar Sa'd refrained from killing him and sent him to Kufa along with the other captives.

When he was brought before Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad, Ibn Ziyad intended to kill him, but due to the mediation of the Asad tribe leaders, Ibn Ziyad exiled him to an area called 'Zurara'. Muraqqa didn't lose his life, but he lost his livelihood in the path of assisting Hussain (AS).
quote: (Ibn Ziyad said to him:) Are you the one who drew a weapon to fight us?... He was exiled for the crime of loving the Imam.
